title: JS Variables
description: Learn to declare variables with let and const, and work with basic data types in JavaScript.

lessons.title: Constants
lessons.description: Use <kbd>const</kbd> to declare a variable that cannot be reassigned. Constants are the default choice for most values in modern JavaScript.
lessons.task: Declare a constant named <kbd>name</kbd> with the value <kbd>"Alice"</kbd>

lessons.title: Let Variables
lessons.description: Use <kbd>let</kbd> to declare variables that you plan to reassign later. Unlike <kbd>const</kbd>, a <kbd>let</kbd> variable can change its value.
lessons.task: Declare a variable <kbd>count</kbd> with <kbd>let</kbd> set to <kbd>0</kbd>, then reassign it to <kbd>5</kbd>

lessons.title: Template Literals
lessons.description: Template literals use backticks <kbd>`</kbd> and <kbd>${}</kbd> to embed expressions inside strings. This makes building dynamic text much easier than string concatenation.
lessons.task: Create a constant <kbd>msg</kbd> using a template literal: <kbd>`Hello, ${name}!`</kbd>

validations.message: Use <kbd>${name}</kbd> inside backticks to embed the variable
Wrap the whole string in backticks <kbd>`</kbd>

lessons.title: Arrays
lessons.description: Arrays store ordered lists of values in square brackets. Access items by index (starting at 0) and use <kbd>.length</kbd> to get the count.
lessons.task: Create a constant <kbd>colors</kbd> with an array: <kbd>["red", "green", "blue"]</kbd>

validations.message: Use square brackets <kbd>[</kbd> to create an array
